title: Aerangis cryptodon
taxonomy: Aerangis cryptodon is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is a genus of orchids that is native to tropical Africa. Aerangis cryptodon specifically is found in the tropical forests of the region. The species belongs to the subtribe Angraecinae, a group of orchids known for their long, slender flowers. Aerangis cryptodon is further classified as a member of the family Orchidaceae, which is one of the largest families of flowering plants.

This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties that may aid in wound healing, respiratory issues, and immune system support.
